title: Tropical Peruvian Pepper Rocoto Relleno
description: A festive, fatty, gooey Peruvian dish where spicy rocoto peppers are stuffed with a sweet pineapple and cheese filling and grilled to perfection.
instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Wash the rocoto peppers and slice off the tops, removing seeds and membranes.
In a bowl, combine quinoa, black beans, pineapple, mozzarella, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper until well mixed.
Spoon the mixture evenly into each pepper cavity, packing gently but firmly.
Brush the outside of each stuffed pepper with olive oil and drizzle pineapple juice over the tops.
Place the peppers on the grill, lid closed, and cook for 8 minutes, turning once, until the peppers soften and the cheese melts.
Remove the peppers from the grill and allow them to rest for 2 minutes so the filling stays intact.
Garnish with a splash of pineapple juice and a sprinkle of fresh chopped cilantro if desired.
Serve the rocoto relleno hot, paired with a side of rice or a simple green salad.
Enjoy the sweet, spicy, and tropical flavors of this festive Peruvian dish.
